originalTitle: Show HN: OpenKnowledge – open source AI-first alternative to Obsidian/Notion
content: Hi HN, Nick here. We’re launching OpenKnowledge ( https://openknowledge.ai/ ), a “what you see is what you get” markdown editor that has direct integrations with Claude, Codex, and other agents. Available as MacOS app or Web UI+CLI. Fully free/local and OSS. We built this because we wanted a Notion-like experience for writing and sharing markdown files across our team. Obsidian is the best alternative we tried, but found it doesn’t have a true WYSWIG UI and it didn’t integrate well with Claude/Codex outside of community plugins. So we built OpenKnowledge. It takes shape as: 1. A MacOS app with a file navigator, the WYSIWYG editor, and link explorer. 2. Integrations with the Claude, Codex, and Cursor desktop apps. The agents can open an OpenKnowledge editor within their embedded web browsers for a side-by-side experience. 3. Built-in mcps, skills, and RAG for LLM-wiki and “AI Second Brain” scenarios + spec writing 4. An embedded terminal and CLI for TUI-first users OSS stack includes: Tiptap/prosemirror, CodeMirror, yjs (CRDT), Electron (MacOS app), Orama, remark/rehype/micromark/mdast, @pierre/trees On the architecture side, the interesting eng. challenges included: 1. A pipeline to convert ProseMirror to markdown in a bidirectional lossless way. ProseMirror uses ASTs, which are not designed to have byte-fidelity. 2. A dual-observer CRDT to keep the ProseMirror and markdown state in-sync. The CRDT + git also power a collaborative experience that shows what Agents are doing in the markdown, have undo/redo, and version history. The “Share” and cloud-sync functionality are geared for team collaboration. They feel “no-code” but leverage git/GitHub under the hood, which also means data stays fully private. In that spirit, we made OpenKnowledge open source for anybody who’s curious or who’d like to contribute. We’re actively thinking about plugins/extensibility and what’s next. If you have suggestions or feedback, would love to hear it.
chineseTitle: Show HN：OpenKnowledge – Obsidian/Notion 的开源 AI 优先替代品
chineseContent: 嗨，HN，尼克在这里。我们正在推出 OpenKnowledge (https://openknowledge.ai/)，这是一个“所见即所得”的 Markdown 编辑器，它与 Claude、Codex 和其他代理直接集成。可用作 MacOS 应用程序或 Web UI+CLI。完全免费/本地和 OSS。我们构建这个是因为我们想要一种类似 Notion 的体验，以便在我们的团队中编写和共享 Markdown 文件。 Obsidian 是我们尝试过的最佳替代方案，但发现它没有真正的 WYSWIG UI，并且与社区插件之外的 Claude/Codex 集成得不好。因此我们构建了 OpenKnowledge。它的形式如下： 1. 带有文件导航器、所见即所得编辑器和链接资源管理器的 MacOS 应用程序。 2. 与 Claude、Codex 和 Cursor 桌面应用程序集成。代理可以在其嵌入式 Web 浏览器中打开 OpenKnowledge 编辑器，以获得并行体验。 3. LLM-wiki 和“AI Second Brain”场景的内置 mcps、技能和 RAG + 规范编写 4. 面向 TUI 优先用户的嵌入式终端和 CLI OSS 堆栈包括：Tiptap/prosemirror、CodeMirror、yjs (CRDT)、Electron (MacOS 应用程序)、Orama、remark/rehype/micromark/mdast、@pierre/trees 在架构方面，有趣的 eng。挑战包括： 1. 以双向无损方式将 ProseMirror 转换为 Markdown 的管道。 ProseMirror 使用 AST，其设计目的并非具有字节保真度。 2. 双观察者 CRDT，用于保持 ProseMirror 和 markdown 状态同步。 CRDT + git 还提供协作体验，显示代理在 markdown 中正在执行的操作、撤消/重做以及版本历史记录。 “共享”和云同步功能适合团队协作。他们感觉“无代码”，但在幕后利用 git/GitHub，这也意味着数据保持完全私密。本着这种精神，我们将 OpenKnowledge 开源给任何有知识的人。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
